item.title: Walmart’s Answer to Apple Pay Wants to Be Your Favorite Financial App
item.description: Backed by the retailer and Ribbit Capital, OnePay is reaching beyond its initial customer base to take on banks.

item.title: Persian Gulf LNG Exporters Are Adopting Shadow-Fleet Tactics
item.description: QatarEnergy and Adnoc are using methods typically employed by Russia to get their gas across the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Swiss 10-Million Cap Damage Can’t Be Easily Dodged, Lombard Says
item.description: The strict limits Switzerland must implement in a proposal to cap its population at 10 million would make economic fallout hard to avoid, according to Lombard Odier.

item.title: Nvidia Is Bigger, Stronger, and Trying to Slay the Laptop Dragon Again
item.description: In its biggest new product for consumers in years, Nvidia is now making PC chips

item.title: Trump Lowers Some US Tariffs in Bid to ‘Spur’ Investment
item.description: The import tax on farm and construction machinery is going down to 15%

item.title: Fed Gets Warning Over Missing the ‘Bigger Picture’ on Inflation
item.description: Christina and David Romer say the Fed needs to be vigilant to keep inflation expectations anchored.

item.title: Trump Premium Supercharges Bond Rallies Across Emerging Markets
item.description: Emerging-market investors are zeroing in on a key metric as they size up the riskiness of a country’s debt: whether its leaders are in good standing with President Donald Trump.

item.title: Palo Alto, CrowdStrike Earnings to Put 37% Cyber Rally to Test
item.description: Investors are about to get a read on the durability of the soaring rally in cybersecurity stocks when two of the industry’s major players report earnings in the coming days.

item.title: Marvell Soars After Nvidia Chief’s $1 Trillion Stock Call
item.description: Marvell Technology Inc. shares surged in early trading Tuesday after Nvidia Corp.’s Jensen Huang predicted the semiconductor and networking company will be the next business to hit a $1 trillion valuation, more than five times its current capitalization.

item.title: A $200 Billion Test of Investors’ Appetite for AI Looms
item.description: Offerings from SpaceX, Anthropic, OpenAI will be blockbuster market events.

item.title: China’s Didi Deepens Losses After Stepping Up Global Investment
item.description: Didi Global Inc. reported its second straight quarterly loss, after ratcheting up investment to expand globally and defend its turf in markets like Brazil from new entrant Meituan.

item.title: Law’s Billable Hour Is Being Shredded by AI
item.description: AI is upending law firms’ billing, hiring and even their legal products.

item.title: Segantii’s Sadler Details Stress at Time of Esprit Trades
item.description: Segantii Capital Management founder Simon Sadler told a Hong Kong court that stresses had been building at home and in his professional life by 2017, the year his firm is accused of trading on inside information.

item.title: Insight Investment Is Lured by 6% Yields Into Battered UK Bonds
item.description: Insight Investment is the latest asset manager to take a punt on long term UK bonds after this year’s selloff, betting that the highest yields in the developed world will be enough to make up for the risks.

item.title: Treasuries Gain Ahead of Week’s First Batch of Employment Data
item.description: Treasuries hovered near a three-week high ahead of this week’s first batch of jobs numbers that could cement the case for the Federal Reserve to deliver an interest-rate hike under new Chair Kevin Warsh.

item.title: India’s Fuel Exports Plunge in May as Domestic Needs Prioritized
item.description: India’s fuel exports tumbled to the lowest level in nearly four years in May as a push to ensure domestic supplies during the Iran war shock along with refinery maintenance curbed overseas flows.

item.title: SoftBank in Early Talks to Back $800 Million Agile Robots Round
item.description: German industrial robotics startup Agile Robots is in discussions to raise about $800 million in new funding from backers including SoftBank Group Corp., as investors seek out companies deploying artificial intelligence in real-world settings.

item.title: Citi’s Chew Sees US Tech Stocks at Risk From Bullish Positioning
item.description: Extended positioning in US technology stocks is putting investors at risk of a reversal, according to Citigroup Inc. strategists.

item.title: PE-Owned JetBio Plans Landmark Green Jet Fuel Plant in Brazil
item.description: Private equity-controlled JetBio plans to build the world’s biggest commercial green jet fuel plant that uses ethanol in Brazil to take advantage of the country’s ample feedstock alternatives.

item.title: Military Antenna Maker Smag Is Said to Weigh Frankfurt IPO
item.description: Smag Mobile Antenna Masts GmbH, a German supplier to the defense industry, is considering an initial public offering in Frankfurt in the coming months, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Korean Firms Turn to Short-Term Funding as Bond Yields Climb
item.description: South Korean firms have increasingly turned to short-term financing as rising government bond yields drive up borrowing costs in the corporate debt market.

item.title: South Africa’s Kganyago Vows to Get Inflation Rate Back to 3%
item.description: South African central bank Governor Lesetja Kganyago promised that officials will lower the inflation rate back to its 3% target despite headwinds from the Iran war.

item.title: Euro-Zone Inflation Surpasses 3% for First Time Since 2023
item.description: Euro-area inflation topped 3% for the first time in more than 2 1/2 years, cementing expectations for an interest-rate hike when the European Central Bank meets next week.

item.title: Galaxy Digital Sets Up Prediction Market OTC Derivatives Desk
item.description: Crypto finance conglomerate Galaxy Digital Inc. has launched a trading desk to offer large investors better access to prediction markets via over-the-counter derivatives similar to those used in markets from interest rates to commodities.

item.title: Fed Warned on Shrinking Balance Sheet in Lookback at Powell Era
item.description: The Federal Reserve should avoid any move to significantly shrink its balance sheet, two prominent economists warned Tuesday — arguing against a strategy that the US central bank’s new chairman has specifically called for.

item.title: Russia Says It Found Foreign Spyware on Top Officials’ Phones
item.description: Russia’s Federal Security Service said it uncovered a large-scale operation by foreign intelligence agencies to implant spyware on senior officials’ mobile phones.

item.title: Swiss Watch Exports Drop, Skewed by 2025 Rush Before US Tariffs
item.description: Swiss watch exports fell in April from a year earlier, when companies were rushing to get stock into the US ahead of higher tariffs.

item.title: UBS Offers First AT1 Bond Since Swiss Authorities Paused Reforms
item.description: UBS Group AG is set to raise Additional Tier 1 capital in US dollars, its first foray into the market since Switzerland paused plans to tighten rules on the riskiest type of bank debt.

item.title: UK Mortgage Approvals Hit 15-Month High Despite Iran War
item.description: UK mortgage approvals unexpectedly rose in April as the housing market displayed ongoing resilience to the economic fallout from war in Iran.

item.title: MediaTek Pledges Hiring Spree to Boost Push Into New AI Spheres
item.description: Taiwanese chipmaker MediaTek Inc. will hike hiring to support a push into new AI activities, joining fellow tech firms in assuaging concerns about job losses in the era of artificial intelligence.
